title: Substack vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack and Campaign Monitor. Substack excels for writers monetizing newsletters; Campaign Monitor suits agencies managing multiple brands.
h1: Substack vs Campaign Monitor: Detailed Comparison
intro: Substack and Campaign Monitor serve different email marketing needs despite both being powerful platforms. Substack is built for individual creators and writers who want to monetize their audience, while Campaign Monitor caters to agencies and businesses managing multiple brands. Your choice depends on whether you're building a direct-to-reader business or managing client campaigns.

comparison_table.tool_b: Advanced automation, analytics, and multi-brand tools

choose_a_if: You're an individual writer or creator looking to monetize directly from subscribers
You want a completely free platform with no subscriber limits
You prefer simplicity and don't need advanced segmentation or automation
choose_b_if: You're an agency or business managing multiple client brands
You need advanced features like journey designer, smart segments, and analytics
You require transactional email capabilities alongside marketing campaigns

faq.q: Is Substack better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Neither is universally better—they solve different problems. Substack excels for individual creators monetizing content, while Campaign Monitor is superior for agencies and businesses needing advanced automation and multi-brand management. Your choice depends on your use case.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Substack is free to start with unlimited subscribers, taking a 10% cut of paid subscription revenue only. Campaign Monitor starts at $11/month with subscriber-based pricing. For pure cost, Substack wins, but Campaign Monitor offers more traditional features for a predictable fee.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your contacts from Campaign Monitor and import them to Substack, though Substack's platform is quite different—it's designed for creators, not agencies. Migration is technically possible but may not suit if you need Campaign Monitor's advanced business features.
